## SECTION 5: KTU 2024 Scheme Examination Question Bank & Topic Recap

### 5.1 Part A Questions (3 Marks Each)

**Q1. [KTU University Exam - May 2024]**
*List any three sectors where Environmental Management Systems are applied and mention one specific environmental aspect relevant to each sector.*

**Model Answer (3 marks):**

1. **Manufacturing Sector** → Environmental Aspect: Emission of particulate matter and SO₂ from boiler stack. *(1 mark)*
2. **IT/ITES Sector** → Environmental Aspect: Generation of e-waste from obsolete computers and peripherals. *(1 mark)*
3. **Healthcare Sector** → Environmental Aspect: Generation of biomedical waste (sharps, pathological waste, chemical disinfectants). *(1 mark)*

---

**Q2. [KTU University Exam - Dec 2023]**
*Define the term "Recycling Rate" in the context of EMS applications. Write its formula.*

**Model Answer (3 marks):**

> **Definition (2 marks):** Recycling Rate is the percentage of total waste generated by an organization that is diverted from disposal and reprocessed into new materials or products, thereby contributing to the circular economy.

> **Formula (1 mark):**

$$
RR = \frac{Waste\ Recycled\ (kg)}{Total\ Waste\ Generated\ (kg)} \times 100\ \%
$$

---

### 5.2 Part B Questions (14 Marks Each — Internal Choice)

> [!WARNING]
> **KTU Examiner's Valuation Pitfall:** Students often answer application questions with **generic theory only** and lose 4–6 marks. To score full 14 marks, you MUST:
> 1. Pick a specific sector (Manufacturing/Hospitality/IT/etc.).
> 2. Identify sector-specific aspects, impacts, and KPIs.
> 3. Show at least one numerical calculation or formula.
> 4. Conclude with a real-world example or case study reference (e.g., "Tata Steel's EMS at Jamshedpur").
> 5. Structure your answer using the ISO 14001:2015 10-clause framework or PDCA cycle.

---

#### **Question A (14 Marks)** — Application Focus: Manufacturing Sector

**[KTU University Exam - May 2023 Model Paper]**

**(a) Apply the ISO 14001:2015 framework to a manufacturing industry. Explain the identification of environmental aspects, evaluation of significance, and setting of objectives. (7 marks)**

**Model Solution:**

**Introduction (1 mark):**
ISO 14001:2015 is the international standard for Environmental Management Systems. Its application in a manufacturing industry (e.g., a textile or steel plant) follows the **Plan-Do-Check-Act (PDCA)** cycle integrated into a 10-clause structure.

**Environmental Aspects Identification (2 marks):**
For each activity, product, or service, environmental aspects are identified. For example:
- **Input aspects:** Raw material extraction, energy consumption, water use.
- **Process aspects:** Air emissions, wastewater discharge, noise, solid waste.
- **Output aspects:** Product disposal, packaging waste, end-of-life vehicle/equipment.

**Significance Evaluation (2 marks):**
Each aspect is scored using a significance matrix:

$$
\text{Significance Score} = (A + M + F)
$$

Where $A$ = severity, $M$ = magnitude/frequency, $F$ = legal factor (each on 1–5 scale). Aspects with score ≥ 10 are classified as **Significant** and require an **Operational Control**.

**Objectives and Targets (2 marks):**
SMART objectives are set for significant aspects, e.g., "Reduce specific water consumption from 8 m³/tonne to 6 m³/tonne by March 2025." Targets are measurable and time-bound.

---

**(b) Discuss the application of EMS in the construction sector with reference to GRIHA rating and construction & demolition (C&D) waste management. (7 marks)**

**Model Solution:**

**Why EMS in Construction (1 mark):**
The construction sector contributes ~25% of India's CO₂ emissions, generates ~150 million tonnes of C&D waste annually, and consumes ~12% of available freshwater. EMS helps mitigate these impacts.

**GRIHA Rating (2 marks):**
GRIHA (Green Rating for Integrated Habitat Assessment) is India's national green building rating system. EMS supports GRIHA by:
- Mandatory environmental management plan at site.
- Documentation of material sourcing, energy use, and waste segregation.
- Indoor air quality and occupant comfort monitoring.

**C&D Waste Management Application (2 marks):**

$$
\text{C\&D Recycling Rate} = \frac{\text{Concrete\ and\ Masonry\ Recycled}}{\text{Total\ C\&D\ Waste\ Generated}} \times 100\ \%
$$

Best practices include:
- On-site segregation of concrete, steel, wood, and plastics.
- Crushing concrete for reuse as aggregate in road sub-base.
- Reuse of steel scrap through authorized recyclers.
- Water sprays for dust suppression and silt fencing for runoff control.

**Conclusion (2 marks):**
The Delhi Metro Rail Corporation (DMRC) and the Lucknow Metro project are exemplary case studies where EMS-driven C&D waste management achieved over **90% recycling rate**, demonstrating the practical viability of EMS in the construction sector.

---

#### **Question B (14 Marks)** — Application Focus: IT/ITES and Hospitality Sectors

**[KTU University Exam - Dec 2022 Model Paper]**

**(a) Explain the application of EMS in the IT/ITES sector. Discuss e-waste management and carbon footprint reduction strategies. (7 marks)**

**Model Solution:**

**Context (1 mark):**
The IT/ITES sector in India employs ~5 million people and contributes ~7% of GDP. Despite being "clean," it generates significant **indirect environmental impacts** through electricity consumption and e-waste.

**E-waste Management Application (3 marks):**
- Implementation of **Extended Producer Responsibility (EPR)** as per the E-Waste Management Rules, 2022.
- Take-back programs for obsolete laptops, servers, and peripherals.
- Partnership with authorized dismantlers and recyclers.
- Asset tracking through RFID/barcode for end-of-life recovery.

**Carbon Footprint Reduction (3 marks):**

$$
CF = \sum_{i=1}^{n} (AD_i \times EF_i)
$$

Where $AD_i$ = activity data (e.g., kWh electricity) and $EF_i$ = emission factor (e.g., 0.82 kgCO₂/kWh for India's grid).

Strategies include:
- Migration to **renewable energy** (solar PPA, wind power).
- **Server virtualization** and cloud computing to reduce hardware.
- **Paperless offices** through digital workflows.
- **Green data centers** with PUE (Power Usage Effectiveness) below 1.5.

---

**(b) Apply EMS to the hospitality industry (hotels and resorts). Highlight at least four key environmental aspects and their control measures. (7 marks)**

**Model Solution:**

**Context (1 mark):**
A 100-room hotel generates ~500 kg waste/day, consumes ~1,500 kWh electricity/day, and uses ~100,000 litres of water/day. EMS is essential for sustainable hospitality operations.

**Four Key Environmental Aspects and Controls (4 marks = 4 × 1 mark):**

| Aspect | Control Measure |
|:-------|:----------------|
| 1. Energy consumption (HVAC, lighting) | LED retrofitting, smart thermostats, solar water heaters |
| 2. Laundry chemicals (detergents, bleach) | Ozonated laundry systems, biodegradable detergents |
| 3. Food waste from kitchens | On-site composting, donation to NGOs, portion control |
| 4. Water consumption in guest rooms | Low-flow fixtures, linen reuse programs, greywater recycling |

**Numerical Example (1 mark):**
If a hotel installs low-flow showerheads reducing flow from 15 L/min to 9 L/min across 100 rooms used 6 hours/day:

$$
\text{Water\ Saved} = (15 - 9) \times 60 \times 6 \times 100 = 216{,}000\ L/day
$$

**Conclusion (1 mark):**
The ITC Hotels chain and Marriott International are global leaders, having achieved **zero waste-to-landfill** status at multiple properties through rigorous EMS application.

---

### 5.3 KTU Examiner's Valuation Warning

> [!WARNING]
> **Common Mark-Loss Zones (Read Carefully Before Exam):**
> 1. **Vague Answers:** Writing only "EMS helps reduce pollution" without specifying aspect, impact, control, and KPI → loses 3–4 marks.
> 2. **Outdated ISO Version:** Referencing ISO 14001:2004 instead of **2015 version** → loses 1 mark.
> 3. **No Numerical Support:** Skipping formulas or numbers in a 14-mark application answer → loses 2–3 marks.
> 4. **Missing Case Study:** Not citing any real-world example (Tata Steel, Infosys, ITC, DMRC, etc.) → loses 1–2 marks.
> 5. **Wrong Sector Mapping:** Listing e-waste for a steel plant or BMW rules for a hotel → loses 2 marks.
> 6. **Forgetting PDCA or 10-Clause Structure:** Without structured framework, the answer is treated as disorganized → loses 2 marks.

---

### 5.4 Topic Recap & Important Things to Remember (Rapid Revision Checklist)

> [!TIP]
> **High-Density Revision Points for Last-Minute Preparation:**

- [x] **EMS Applications** = Practical implementation of ISO 14001:2015 in any sector.
- [x] **Core Sectors:** Manufacturing, Construction, IT/ITES, Hospitality, Healthcare, Mining, Transport, Municipal.
- [x] **ISO 14001:2015** has a **10-clause structure** (Clauses 4–10 are mandatory for EMS).
- [x] **PDCA Cycle** = Plan → Do → Check → Act (the operational engine of every EMS).
- [x] **Environmental Aspect** = Element of an organization's activity that can interact with the environment.
- [x] **Environmental Impact** = Any change to the environment, whether adverse or beneficial.
- [x] **Significance Evaluation Formula:** Score = $A + M + F$ (each on 1–5 scale, ≥ 10 = Significant).
- [x] **Recycling Rate Formula:** $RR = \frac{\text{Waste\ Recycled}}{\text{Total\ Waste\ Generated}} \times 100$.
- [x] **Specific Energy Consumption (SEC):** $SEC = \frac{\text{Total\ Energy\ (MJ)}}{\text{Production\ Output\ (tonnes)}}$.
- [x] **Specific Water Consumption (SWC):** $SWC = \frac{\text{Total\ Water\ (m}^3\text{)}}{\text{Production\ Output\ (tonnes)}}$.
- [x] **Carbon Footprint Formula:** $CF = \sum (AD_i \times EF_i)$ in tCO₂e.
- [x] **Waste Intensity:** $WI = \frac{\text{Total\ Waste\ (kg)}}{\text{Production\ Output\ (units)}}$.
- [x] **EPI Formula:** $EPI = \frac{\text{Actual}}{\text{Target}} \times 100\%$.
- [x] **BOD Removal Efficiency:** $\eta = \frac{BOD_{in} - BOD_{out}}{BOD_{in}} \times 100\%$.
- [x] **IMS (Integrated Management System)** = EMS + QMS (ISO 9001) + OHS (ISO 45001) + EnMS (ISO 50001).
- [x] **EPR (Extended Producer Responsibility)** is mandatory under India's E-Waste Rules 2022.
- [x] **BMW (Biomedical Waste) Management Rules 2016 & 2018 amendments** govern healthcare EMS.
- [x] **GRIHA** = India's green building rating (15–100 points); **LEED** = International counterpart.
- [x] **Continual Improvement** is a mandatory requirement of Clause 10 of ISO 14001:2015.
- [x] **Top Management** must demonstrate **leadership and commitment** (Clause 5) — this is the most-tested area.
- [x] **Real-World Examples to Quote:** Tata Steel (Jamshedpur), Infosys (Mysore campus, 100% renewable), ITC Hotels (zero waste), DMRC (C&D recycling), Reliance Industries (Jamnagar refinery EMS).

> [!IMPORTANT]
> **Final KTU 2024 Exam Mantra:**
> "When asked to **apply** EMS → always use: **Context → Aspects → Impacts → Significance → Objectives → Controls → Monitoring → Review → Improvement** with **at least one formula and one real-world example**."
